macro_segments.title: The Debate Between Beit Shammai and Beit Hillel on the Order of Blessings at a Meal: Kiddush and Wine

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Debate: Which Blessing Comes First - Mekadesh HaShabbat or Borei Peri HaGafen
Beit Shammai's Position: The Day Comes First Because It Causes the Wine
Beit Hillel's Position: Wine First Because It Enables Kiddush, and the Tadir Principle
The Halakhah Follows Beit Hillel, with Discussion of the Heavenly Voice (Bas Kol)
Explanation of Why We Always Rule Like Beit Hillel: The Majority vs. Intelligence Debate
Rabbi Yehoshua's Rejection of the Heavenly Voice: Lo BaShamayim Hi
Tosafot's Resolution: The Bas Kol Was Determining Procedure, Not Overriding the Majority

macro_segments.title: Examination of Havdalah and Its Apparent Contradiction to Kiddush in the Order of Blessings

micro_segments.title: The Baraita on Havdalah: Starting with Wine, Then Spices, Then the Blessing
Is This Baraita Beit Shammai or Beit Hillel? Using Rabbi Yehuda's Framework
Proving the Baraita Represents Beit Shammai: The Order of Esh Before Besamim
The Question: Why Does Beit Shammai Start Havdalah with Wine but Not Kiddush?
Beit Shammai's Answer: Coming Into the Day vs. Going Out - Timing and Urgency
The Symbolism: Eagerness to Begin Shabbat, Reluctance to End It

macro_segments.title: The Problem of Eating Before Havdalah and the Importance of Birkat HaMazon Al HaKos

micro_segments.title: The Baraita: One Cup Scenario - Eating Before Havdalah to Recite Birkat HaMazon Over Wine
Tosafot's Analysis: Eating Before Havdalah Is Permitted to Enable Birkat HaMazon Al HaKos
Implications for the Requirement of Birkat HaMazon Al HaKos: One Person, Three, or Ten?
Alternative Solutions: Using a Defective Cup (Pagum) or Adding Water

macro_segments.title: The Debate in the Mishna on Wine After a Meal and the Two Approaches to Birkat HaMazon

micro_segments.title: The Mishna: One Cup of Wine After the Meal - Beit Shammai vs. Beit Hillel
Interpreting the Mishna: Does Beit Shammai Require Birkat HaMazon Al HaKos?
The Gemara's Analysis: Tasting and the Pagum Issue - Dipping vs. Drinking

macro_segments.title: The Requirement for a Minimum Amount in the Cup and Adding Wine or Water

micro_segments.title: The Problem: If Only One Cup Exists, How Can It Have More Than a Minimum Amount?
The Resolution: The Cup Is Larger Than One Minimum but Smaller Than Two
Practical Application: Dipping a Finger for Taste While Maintaining the Kos Requirement

macro_segments.title: The Debate in the Mishna on Wine After a Meal and the Two Approaches to Birkat HaMazon (continued)

micro_segments.title: The Baraita: Beit Shammai Allows Drinking the Wine and Then Birkat HaMazon
The Gemara's Resolution: Beit Shammai May Not Require Birkat HaMazon Al HaKos

macro_segments.title: The Requirement for a Minimum Amount in the Cup and Adding Wine or Water (continued)

micro_segments.title: Tosafot on Fixing a Pagum Cup: Adding Wine or Water to Restore Its Status

macro_segments.title: The Asymmetry of Blessing Order: Why Kiddush and Havdalah Begin with Wine but Birkat HaMazon Ends with It

micro_segments.title: The Question: Why Is the Order of Blessings Different for Kiddush vs. Birkat HaMazon?
Tosafot's Explanation: Birkat HaMazon Represents Completion, Creating a Hefsek from Drinking
Alternative Reason: The Psychological Impact of Declaring the Meal Over
Why Kiddush and Havdalah Are Different: The Intention to Eat or Drink Afterward
Tosafot's Concern About Infinite Birkat HaMazon: If Every Wine Requires Blessing Al HaKos

macro_segments.title: Ritual Purity Laws (Tumah V'Taharah): Hand Washing Before vs. After Wine in a Meal

micro_segments.title: Beit Shammai: Wash Hands First, Then Pour Wine - Preventing the Cup from Becoming a Sheni
The Concern: Unwashed Hands (Sheni) Will Make Wine Droplets a Rishon, Which Makes the Cup a Sheni
Beit Hillel: Pour Wine First, Then Wash Hands - Allowing Use of Sheni Vessels
Beit Hillel's Logic: The Vessel Can Be Sheni; Concern Is Instead About the Vessel Making Hands Tamei
Gemara Analysis: Why Liquids Are the Conduit - Vessels Cannot Directly Affect Vessels or People
The Mishna: Only Outside of Vessel Can Be Tamei When Touched by Liquid - Inside Remains Tahor
Fundamental Disagreement: Whether Using a Sheni Vessel Is Permitted in a Meal
Beit Hillel's Additional Reason (Davar Acher): Hand Washing Should Directly Precede Bread

macro_segments.title: Ritual Purity Laws: Wiping Hands on a Towel - Table vs. Couch Placement

micro_segments.title: Beit Shammai: Place Towel on Table - Avoiding the Couch Contaminating the Towel's Liquids
Beit Shammai's Concern: A Tamei Couch Will Make Liquids on Towel a Rishon, Then Hands Sheni
Beit Hillel: Keep Towel on Couch - Table Contaminating Food Through Towel Liquids
Beit Hillel's Concern: A Tamei Table (Sheni) Will Make Towel Liquids a Rishon, Contaminating Food
Gemara Analysis: Why a Sheni Vessel Cannot Directly Affect Food - Liquids Are Necessary
Beit Hillel's Argument: Preferring Hand Contamination Over Food Contamination - All Rabbinic Anyway
